<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Documentation is fully decoupled (coupling 0.0) and self-contained, which is correct — docs should never be imported by source code.
- The concentration of memory-architecture and memory-*-analysis docs suggests the memory/context system is an active area of design investment; keeping these docs current as the system evolves will be important.
- The mix of time-stamped analysis docs (2026-03-03-*) alongside evergreen reference docs (level-system-reference.md) is a useful pattern for separating temporal notes from stable architecture records.
- Timestamped analysis files (e.g. 2026-03-03-refresh-orchestration-memory-analysis.md) sitting alongside evergreen docs is good practice — it preserves decision history without polluting the stable reference docs.
- Eight or more memory-focused documents indicates the memory/context architecture is complex and actively evolving; consider a single index doc to orient new readers across the memory-*.md files.
